evidence_policy.promotion_rule: No model, dataset, retrieval component, or release is promoted from this portfolio until every applicable blocking gate has a reproducible local receipt tied to an immutable upstream revision and artifact hash.

labels.PRIMARY_REPORTED: A fact reported by the named upstream project's official repository, model card, specification, or paper; it is not a local measurement.
labels.ENGINEERING_INFERENCE: A bounded engineering judgment that must be tested on the target host before use or promotion.
labels.DECLARED_PLAN: A proposed SZL activity or artifact that this manifest does not claim has been executed.
labels.LOCAL_MEASURED_REQUIRED: A promotion condition that requires a reproducible local run and retained evidence.
labels.NOT_MEASURED: No qualifying local receipt is attached to this manifest.

target_laptop_profile.declared_target_class: Single Windows laptop GPU in the approximately 8 GB VRAM class
constraints: Run an on-host preflight before downloading or loading any candidate weights.
Start with a bounded 8192-token or 16384-token context rather than treating an upstream maximum context as a local operating envelope.
Measure cold load, steady-state peak VRAM, first-token latency, generation throughput, thermals, restart behavior, and out-of-memory behavior.
Pin upstream revisions and record tokenizer, configuration, and weight hashes before training or evaluation.
Do not run concurrent training jobs or assume that free remote inference is free training compute.
Prefer adapter training over full-parameter training unless a measured resource receipt justifies a different method.

primary_reported_facts: The official repository identifies the artifact as a four-bit Qwen2.5 1.5B Instruct derivative.
The official repository reports Apache-2.0 licensing.
This is the upstream base already named by the current ReceiptAgent program; this portfolio does not claim its adapter has passed release gates.

required_preflight: Preserve and verify the existing pinned upstream revision and all tokenizer, configuration, and weight hashes.
Measure clean model and adapter load on the target host without fallback substitution.
Measure peak VRAM, step time, thermals, checkpoint recovery, and out-of-memory behavior with the intended adapter recipe.
Run held-out receipt-schema validity, citation integrity, refusal, and replay-determinism evaluations.

promotion_scope: Finish the existing adapter qualification before starting a replacement training program.
Publish as experimental only after identity, rights, contamination, task-quality, safety, resource, restart, and receipt-integrity gates pass.

models.role: Compact multimodal generalist candidate for governed Forge tasks
primary_reported_facts: The official model card reports a 2B language model with a vision encoder.
The official model card reports native 262144-token context support.
The official model card reports Apache-2.0 licensing; its reported context limit is not a measured laptop operating point.

required_preflight: Pin a concrete upstream revision and verify the exact processor, tokenizer, configuration, and weight hashes.
Prove the selected runtime supports the model architecture and multimodal processor without silent fallback.
Measure quantized cold load, peak VRAM, latency, throughput, thermals, bounded-context behavior, and restart determinism.
Attempt adapter training only after an isolated short-run feasibility test and held-out evaluation plan are approved.

promotion_scope: Inference bakeoff against the existing ReceiptAgent base and text-only controls.
No training or product routing until the target laptop produces qualifying local receipts.

models.role: Text-only compact generalist and adapter-training control
primary_reported_facts: The official model card identifies a 1.7B-parameter causal language model.
The official model card documents switchable thinking and non-thinking modes.
The official model card reports Apache-2.0 licensing.

required_preflight: Pin and hash the upstream revision, tokenizer, configuration, and weights.
Measure quantized and supported training-precision load behavior on the target host.
Measure peak VRAM, step time, thermals, checkpoint restart, and out-of-memory behavior in a bounded adapter smoke run.
Evaluate thinking-mode policy, receipt-schema validity, grounded abstention, and latency separately.

promotion_scope: Serve as a text-only control for the compact generalist bakeoff.
Qualify for adapter training only if it beats or complements the existing base on preregistered Forge tasks.

models.role: Enterprise RAG, code, and function-calling control
primary_reported_facts: The official model card identifies a 2B instruction-tuned model.
The official model card describes intended use for retrieval-augmented generation, function calling, code, and long-context tasks.
The official model card reports Apache-2.0 licensing.

required_preflight: Pin and hash the exact upstream revision and artifacts.
Measure supported quantized load, peak VRAM, latency, throughput, thermals, bounded context, and restart behavior.
Validate tool-call schemas, citation behavior, and refusal semantics without relying on upstream benchmark summaries.
Treat any adapter training as unproven until an isolated local smoke run and resource receipt pass.

promotion_scope: Control for tool use, RAG, and enterprise-style instruction adherence.
Remain a bakeoff candidate unless it produces superior local evidence for a defined Forge role.

primary_reported_facts: The official model card identifies a 3B-parameter language model.
The official model card reports training on 11.2 trillion tokens and links training resources.
The official model card reports Apache-2.0 licensing.

required_preflight: Pin and hash the exact upstream revision and artifacts.
Measure quantized load, peak VRAM, latency, throughput, thermals, bounded-context behavior, and restart determinism.
Compare task quality per unit latency and memory against the smaller controls.
Do not schedule training on the target laptop unless a separate measured resource review approves it.

promotion_scope: Inference-only challenger in the compact generalist bakeoff.
Exclude from the initial laptop training queue.

models.role: Code, patch, and structured repository-task specialist
primary_reported_facts: The official model card identifies an instruction-tuned 1.5B code model.
The official model card describes code generation, reasoning, and fixing capabilities.
The official model card reports Apache-2.0 licensing.

required_preflight: Pin and hash the exact upstream revision, tokenizer, configuration, and weights.
Measure load, peak VRAM, step time, thermals, checkpoint restart, and out-of-memory behavior.
Evaluate patch applicability, test preservation, secret refusal, and hallucinated-file rates on held-out repositories.
Prove training/evaluation repository separation and deny benchmark test leakage.

promotion_scope: Specialist adapter only; do not market it as a universal generalist.
Require executable patch and test receipts before promotion.

models.role: Mathematical reasoning and proof-obligation inference challenger
primary_reported_facts: The official model card identifies a 3.8B-parameter reasoning model.
The official model card scopes the model to mathematical reasoning.
The official model card reports MIT licensing.

required_preflight: Pin and hash the exact upstream revision and artifacts.
Measure quantized load, peak VRAM, latency, throughput, thermals, and restart behavior.
Score proof-obligation validity with Lean or another independent checker where a formal target exists.
Separate mathematical correctness from fluent explanation and record every counterexample or failed verification.

promotion_scope: Math-only inference challenger for proof-obligation drafting and critique.
Never label generated text as a proof unless an independent checker and retained receipt validate it.

models.role: Instruction-aware multilingual dense retrieval component for the second Brain
primary_reported_facts: The official model card identifies a 0.6B embedding model with a reported 32768-token context length.
The official model card reports user-selectable embedding dimensions from 32 through 1024 using Matryoshka representation learning.
The official model card reports instruction-aware support across more than 100 languages and Apache-2.0 licensing.

required_preflight: Pin and hash the exact upstream revision, tokenizer, configuration, and weights.
Measure load, peak VRAM or RAM, embedding latency, batch throughput, thermals, and restart behavior.
Evaluate recall at multiple dimensions and bounded context sizes on a versioned SZL-BrainQrels split.
Verify language, formula, code, and citation retrieval separately; do not infer quality from upstream leaderboards.

promotion_scope: Dense candidate generation in a hybrid second-Brain retrieval pipeline.
No index migration until local recall, latency, memory, and reindex receipts pass.

models.role: Instruction-aware candidate reranking component for the second Brain
primary_reported_facts: The official model card identifies a 0.6B text reranking model.
The official model card reports a 32768-token context length and instruction-aware multilingual support.
The official model card reports Apache-2.0 licensing.

required_preflight: Pin and hash the exact upstream revision, tokenizer, configuration, and weights.
Measure load, peak VRAM or RAM, per-candidate latency, batch throughput, thermals, and restart behavior.
Evaluate nDCG, MRR, grounded answer rate, and abstention changes on a versioned SZL-BrainQrels split.
Prove that reranking cannot admit quarantined or rights-blocked evidence.

promotion_scope: Rerank only already-admitted hybrid retrieval candidates.
No production routing until quality gains exceed the measured latency and resource cost.

models.role: Independent input and output safety classification candidate
primary_reported_facts: The official model card identifies a 0.6B generative safety model.
The official model card describes classification of user messages and assistant responses across safety categories and severity levels.
The official model card reports Apache-2.0 licensing.

required_preflight: Pin and hash the exact upstream revision, tokenizer, configuration, and weights.
Measure load, peak VRAM or RAM, classification latency, throughput, thermals, and restart behavior.
Evaluate false-negative and false-positive rates on a rights-cleared SZL-PolicyAdversarial split.
Keep deterministic policy rules and human approval authoritative; the classifier is not a policy oracle.

promotion_scope: Independent safety signal in a defense-in-depth pipeline.
Never use the model as the sole authorization, release, or execution gate.

collections.role: Versioned first-party benchmark and training collection for receipt schemas, tool calls, refusals, provenance, and replay determinism
admission_gates: Record source URL, immutable revision, content hash, retrieval timestamp, and derivation for every admitted row.
Attach rights evidence and complete row-level or derivation review before use.
Scan for PII, credentials, secrets, signed URLs, and production identifiers.
Run exact, fuzzy, and semantic deduplication before deterministic splitting.
Apply a benchmark contamination denylist and prove that evaluation rows cannot enter training splits.
Validate every schema-bearing example with the declared machine-readable schema.
required_release_artifacts: Dataset card with intended use, limitations, composition, collection process, and evaluation exclusions
Content-addressed row manifest and deterministic split manifest
forbidden_content: Secrets, tokens, credentials, or signed URLs
Raw signed production receipts or private execution traces
Benchmark test data or data derived from benchmark answers
Rows with unknown or conflicting rights
Synthetic rows that are not explicitly labeled with generator, prompt, revision, and verifier provenance

collections.role: Human-reviewed queries, relevance judgments, hard negatives, citations, abstention cases, and graph routes for second-Brain evaluation
admission_gates: Admit only canonical Brain artifacts with source URL or local origin, immutable revision, hash, and timestamp.
Exclude quarantine, stale-without-label, rights-blocked, secret-bearing, and unverifiable nodes.
Use independent review for relevance labels and record adjudication disagreements.
Run exact, fuzzy, and semantic deduplication across queries, positives, and negatives.
Freeze deterministic train, development, and test splits by source family to prevent near-duplicate leakage.
Require explicit unanswerable and abstention examples rather than forcing every query to have an answer.
required_release_artifacts: W3C PROV-compatible evidence and judgment lineage
forbidden_content: Quarantined Brain nodes
Secrets, credentials, raw private conversations, or private human case data
Claims without a retained source or first-party artifact hash
Benchmark test documents or answer keys
Unknown-rights documents

collections.role: Formula-ID crosswalk, executable examples, counterexamples, proof obligations, checker receipts, and uncertainty labels
admission_gates: Assign stable formula and obligation IDs and retain exact source, revision, hash, and license evidence.
Separate definitions, conjectures, proved statements, falsified statements, heuristics, and empirical observations.
Require an executable reference implementation or formal statement when the obligation type permits it.
Validate claimed proofs with Lean or another independent checker and retain failure output; prose alone is not proof.
Run contamination checks against held-out math evaluations and prevent solution leakage across splits.
Deduplicate symbolic normal forms, natural-language paraphrases, and generated derivations.
required_release_artifacts: Dataset card with epistemic labels and checker coverage
Formula-ID crosswalk and W3C PROV-compatible lineage
Content-addressed statements, implementations, and checker receipts
forbidden_content: Unverified language-model output labeled as a proof
Secrets or proprietary source material without redistribution rights
Benchmark answer keys in training splits
Formulas with missing identity, source, status, or units where units apply
Unknown-rights rows

collections.role: Rights-cleared prompt-injection, unsafe-content, policy-conflict, exfiltration, and refusal evaluation collection
admission_gates: Pin source revisions and record hashes, retrieval time, rights evidence, and transformations.
Perform row-level safety, privacy, and redistribution review before admission.
Scan for real credentials, secrets, personal data, malware payloads, and executable exploit content.
Deduplicate exact, fuzzy, semantic, and templated adversarial examples.
Keep evaluation attacks and templates isolated from training data.
Record policy version, label taxonomy, reviewer agreement, and disputed labels.
required_release_artifacts: Dataset card with hazard handling and access limitations
W3C PROV-compatible source and annotation lineage
forbidden_content: Live credentials, secrets, tokens, or signed URLs
Actionable malware or exploit material outside an approved controlled corpus
Unredacted personal data or private case records
Benchmark test attacks in training splits
Unknown-rights rows

collections.role: Held-out repository tasks for patch correctness, test preservation, secret refusal, provenance, and executable receipts
admission_gates: Retain repository URL, immutable revision, license evidence, file hashes, task derivation, and test provenance.
Admit only repositories and generated rows whose rights permit the intended use and redistribution.
Scan all snapshots and diffs for secrets, credentials, personal data, generated binaries, and malicious content.
Split by repository and upstream lineage to prevent file-level and near-duplicate leakage.
Exclude benchmark test sets and private repositories from training.
Require sandboxed executable tests and preserve stdout, stderr, exit code, resource, and patch receipts.
required_release_artifacts: Dataset card with repository and language coverage
W3C PROV-compatible repository, task, patch, and test lineage
Contamination, secret-scan, license, and execution report
forbidden_content: Private source code without explicit authorization
Secrets, credentials, signed artifacts, or production configuration
Benchmark test solutions in training splits
Generated patches without source and verifier provenance
Unknown or incompatible license content

collections.role: Append-only, content-addressed model identity, environment, training, evaluation, safety, latency, energy, and release receipts
admission_gates: Accept only schema-valid receipts bound to a model revision, dataset revision, code revision, environment fingerprint, and artifact hashes.
Cryptographically hash every receipt and preserve signing or attestation identity where available.
Reject mutable URLs, hand-entered benchmark numbers, missing units, missing timestamps, or missing command provenance.
Redact secrets and personal data while preserving a verifiable digest of protected material when permitted.
Separate vendor-reported, local-measured, inferred, and unknown fields in every receipt.
Detect replay, conflicting identity, and metric substitution before release admission.
required_release_artifacts: Dataset card describing receipt schemas and trust boundaries
W3C PROV-compatible execution and artifact lineage
Schema, redaction, conflict, and replay audit report
forbidden_content: Secrets, access tokens, credentials, or signed URLs
Raw private prompts, user data, or production payloads
Hand-entered performance numbers without machine evidence
Receipts whose model, data, code, or environment identity cannot be resolved
Rewritten or deleted historical receipts outside a separately attested correction event

stages: Retain immutable source bytes and content hashes before transformation.
Use Docling or another pinned parser to produce normalized text, layout, tables, formulas, images, and parser receipts.
Apply rights, provenance, freshness, secret, privacy, quarantine, and canonical-identity admission gates.
Build independently versioned lexical, dense, entity, relation, and community indexes.
Generate hybrid candidates with BM25 or equivalent lexical retrieval plus Qwen3-Embedding-0.6B dense retrieval after local qualification.
Expand admitted candidates through bounded graph neighborhoods with explicit edge provenance.
Rerank only admitted candidates with Qwen3-Reranker-0.6B after local qualification.
Route to a governed generator with citation requirements, policy gates, and a fail-closed abstention path.
Record retrieval, generation, verification, memory-write, latency, resource, and outcome receipts.

query_routes.direct_fact: Hybrid lexical and dense retrieval, bounded rerank, source-level citation, and abstention when evidence is insufficient.
query_routes.entity_relation: Hybrid retrieval followed by local provenance-bounded graph traversal; every returned edge must retain source and revision identity.
query_routes.corpus_sensemaking: GraphRAG-style local and global community summaries generated only from admitted evidence, with summaries treated as derived evidence rather than source truth.
query_routes.investigative: DRIFT-style iterative retrieval with a fixed budget, explicit hypotheses, conflict tracking, stop conditions, and a complete query trail.

invariants: Original bytes and normalized derivatives remain independently content-addressed.
Quarantined, rights-blocked, stale-without-label, secret-bearing, or unverifiable evidence cannot enter retrieval or memory writes.
Every answer claim cites admitted chunk, source, revision, and retrieval receipt; graph-derived claims also cite edge provenance.
The system abstains when evidence coverage, source identity, freshness, or conflict resolution is insufficient.
Memory writes require policy admission, provenance, confidence, conflict analysis, and a reversible correction trail.
Generated summaries and embeddings never replace the canonical source artifact.
Query budgets, model revisions, index revisions, parser revisions, and all fallbacks are visible in the receipt.

promotion_gates.requirement: Resolve an immutable upstream revision and locally verify tokenizer, processor, configuration, and every weight-file hash; refuse promotion on mismatch or substitution.
Produce model, dataset, code, and derivative rights evidence with source revisions, row-level or derivation lineage, redistribution analysis, and a retained reviewer decision.
Run exact, fuzzy, semantic, source-family, and benchmark-denylist contamination tests and retain deterministic split manifests proving evaluation isolation.
Pass preregistered held-out task metrics and baselines for the named model role, including failure cases, confidence intervals where appropriate, and no hand-entered scores.
Pass role-specific refusal, prompt-injection, exfiltration, unsafe-content, privilege, and policy-conflict evaluations with retained false-negative and false-positive evidence.
Measure versioned qrels recall, MRR or nDCG, citation precision, grounded answer rate, conflict surfacing, and abstention behavior for every enabled retrieval route.
Measure cold load, peak VRAM and RAM, first-token latency, throughput, energy or power where observable, thermals, bounded-context behavior, and out-of-memory failure on the declared target host.
Demonstrate clean restart, checkpoint recovery where applicable, deterministic identity revalidation, index recovery, and fail-closed behavior when any required dependency is absent.
Validate append-only schema-correct receipts bound to model, data, code, environment, command, timestamp, units, outputs, and artifact hashes; test replay and conflicting-identity rejection.

primary_sources.title: From Local to Global: A Graph RAG Approach to Query-Focused Summarization
The Data Provenance Initiative: A Large Scale Audit of Dataset Licensing and Attribution in AI
Language Model Evaluation Harness task guide
Data Cards: Purposeful and Transparent Dataset Documentation for Responsible AI
Aegis AI Content Safety Dataset 2.0 card
